Notice Of Motion Compelling (Further) Responses To Pltf'S Form Interrogatories And For Award Of Monetary Sanctios
Off Calendar Jul-10-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Beth Mora, appeared for Plaintiff/Moving party
Jane Trigero, for Defendant/Opposing party TPMG
Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted with modifications: Plaintiff Barbara Campbell's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories is granted.
The response to No. 4.1 is inadequate. Defendant is ordered to respond further.
The objections to Nos. 12.2, 12.3, and 12.6 are overruled. Defendant is ordered to respond further as the responses are inadequate.
The response to No. 15.1 is inadequate.
Further responses shall be made within 20 days of notice of this order.
The court finds that the responses were a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are imposed against Defendant in the amount of $900, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order.
MODIFICATIONS: Defendant retains the right to designated documents as private and limiting facilities to the 9 county Bay Area facilities. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Notice Of Motion Compelling Production Of Documents And Of Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-10-2006 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Deft For Appointment Of A Referee
Off Calendar Jul-10-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Beth Mora, appeared for Plaintiff/Moving party
Jane Trigero, for Defendant/Opposing party TPMG
Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted with modifications: Defendant The Permanente Medical Group's Motion For Relief From Any Purported Waiver Of Objections To Interrogatories and Requests For Production Of Documents, and Request For A Protective Order is granted as it seeks relief from waiver of attorney client and attorney work product privilege. Relief is also granted as to privacy objections to the extent they are recognized by orders made in motions being heard at the same time as this motion. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied.
MODIFICATIONS: Defendant retains the right to designated documents as private and limiting facilities to the 9 county Bay Area facilities. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Notice Of Motion Compelling (Further) Responses To Form Interrogatories-Emplyment Law And For Award On Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-10-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Beth Mora, appeared for Plaintiff/Moving party
Jane Trigero, for Defendant/Opposing party TPMG
Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted with modifications: Plaintiff Barbara Campbell's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories-Employment Law is granted in part and denied in part.
The motion is granted as to Nos. 200.1, 200.4, 201.3, 207.1, 207.2, 208.1, 208.2, 211.2, 214.1, and 216.1. The responses are inadequate.
The motion is denied as to No. 200.5. The response is adequate.
The motion is granted as to No. 209.2, limited to Departments of Psychiatry in facilities in the Northern California region, and limited to complaints of race and gender discrimination.
The motion is granted as to Nos. 215.1 and 215.2. The objections are overruled. Defendant is ordered to respond further.
Further responses shall be made within 20 days of notice of this order.
The court finds that the responses were a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are imposed against Defendant in the amount of $1,200, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order.
MODIFICATIONS: Defendant retains the right to designated documents as private and limiting facilities to the 9 county Bay Area facilities. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
